description_long: This is a classic submachine gun with a wooden fixed stock and a long, perforated metal barrel shroud. The receiver and barrel are constructed from dark, blued steel, while the stock and grip are made from polished wood with visible grain and a metal buttplate. The rifle features a prominent drum magazine beneath the receiver, characteristic of mid-20th-century military firearms. Several spent brass cartridges are depicted ejecting from the action, suggesting the weapon is in the process of firing. Iron sights are mounted along the top of the barrel and receiver, and the overall design is robust and utilitarian, suitable for rugged urban or battlefield environments.
description: A vintage submachine gun with a wooden stock, metal receiver, and a drum magazine, shown ejecting spent cartridges. The rifle has a perforated barrel shroud and iron sights.
description_view_0: Side view showing the wooden stock, metal receiver, drum magazine, and ejected cartridges.
description_view_1: Front-on view highlighting the circular drum magazine, perforated barrel shroud, and muzzle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the wooden stock, metal receiver, drum magazine, and ejected cartridges.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the wooden stock, top of the receiver, and the alignment of the iron sights.
